mission.title: kubeflow: Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application
mission.description: Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application. Users encounter: "couldn't apply KfApp:  (kubeflow.error): Code 500 with message: kfApp Ap".

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `couldn't apply KfApp:  (kubeflow.error): Code 500 with message: kfApp Apply failed for kustomize:  (kubeflow.error): Cod`

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
I ran `kfctl apply k8s` a 2nd time and it failed saying istio had already bound the port.

output:

> time="2019-08-01T14:24:56-07:00" level=info msg="cr

steps.title: Apply the fix for Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application
steps.description:
+1 to that. I think coloring the did to apply is getting hairy and will end
up replicating kubectl.

On Fri, Aug 9, 2019 at 1:55 PM Matt Brown <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Has it ever been considered to shell out to kubectl apply ... rather than
> have client-go code in kfctl that talks to t
```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
namespace: kf-demo
resources:
- https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/blob/master/pipeline/pipelines-ui/base/kustomization.yaml
generators:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joe/pipelines/app.yaml
```

steps.title: Confirm Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `couldn't apply KfApp:  (kubeflow.error): Code 500 with message: kfApp Apply failed for kustomize:  (kubeflow.error): Cod` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: +1 to that. I think coloring the did to apply is getting hairy and will end
up replicating kubectl.

On Fri, Aug 9, 2019 at 1:55 PM Matt Brown <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Has it ever been considered to shell out to kubectl apply ... rather than
> have client-go code in kfctl that talks to the k8s api-server directly?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to th. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
namespace: kf-demo
resources:
- https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/blob/master/pipeline/pipelines-ui/base/kustomization.yaml
generators:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joe/pipelines/app.yaml

apiVersion: kfdef.apps.kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
kind: KfDef
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  name: dls-kf
  namespace: kubeflow
spec:
  appdir: /Users/joe/kf
  applications:
  - kustomizeConfig:
      overlays:
      - istio
      repoRef:
        name: manifests
        path: pipeline/pipelines-ui
    name: pipelines-ui

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
